extraction.one_line_summary: LAMPAERT Yves won solo after Team Sky forced echelon chaos 8km from finish, Quick-Step caught and countered at 60kph.
tactical_patterns: Team Sky's echelon attempt at 8km out on flat roads backfired when Quick-Step's superior TTT skills turned defense into winning offense.
DENNIS Rohan took bonus seconds at intermediate sprint to virtually claim red jersey, showing Quick-Step targeted GC gaps before finale.
Sky rolling at 60kph with 8km remaining on flat stage signals they'll weaponize echelons aggressively even without crosswinds.
Quick-Step converting Sky's failed echelon into LAMPAERT solo proves Belgian squad's superior collective strength in nervous finales.

rider_signals.LAMPAERT Yves: positive: converted defensive Quick-Step response to Sky's echelon into irresistible solo, taking stage and both jerseys
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: mechanical problems mid-stage but Team Sky's 8km echelon attempt shows aggressive GC tactics despite stage profile
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: crashed out of race on Stage 2, ending Vuelta before mountains
rider_signals.MORENO Javier: negative: crashed out of race on Stage 2 alongside teammate NIBALI
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: took intermediate sprint bonuses to virtually claim red jersey before Quick-Step's finale dominance
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: finished second behind teammate LAMPAERT, showing Quick-Step's depth in controlling chaotic finale
rider_signals.MODOLO Sacha: neutral: took fifth despite Quick-Step controlling finale, indicating sprint positioning survived echelon chaos
